data.change_summary: Replaced the silent .ok() in snapshot_plan_hash() (fs.rs:2570) with an explicit match that distinguishes file-not-found (returns None silently, as expected for milestones without a snapshot) from all other errors (corrupt JSON, permission failures, etc.), which now emit a tracing::warn with the milestone_id and error details before returning None. This ensures operators can detect corrupted snapshots that silently prevent plan_hash auto-population, without changing the function's return type or caller behavior.
outstanding_risks: Pre-existing formatting drift in tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs is unrelated to these changes but will appear in cargo fmt --check across the full workspace.

steps.description: Replaced .ok().and_then(|s| s.plan_hash) in snapshot_plan_hash() with a match expression: Ok(snapshot) extracts plan_hash, Err(AppError::Io(NotFound)) returns None silently, all other Err variants log tracing::warn with milestone_id and error before returning None
Updated doc comment on snapshot_plan_hash() to document the new warning behavior for unexpected errors
Ran cargo test (683 unit + 351 lib + 128 integration + 1 doc = 1163 total, 0 failed), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check on src/adapters/fs.rs (clean)

validation_evidence: cargo test: 683 unit tests passed, 0 failed; 1163 total across all test targets, 0 failed
cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
cargo fmt --check: src/adapters/fs.rs clean; only pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs
Match arm Err(AppError::Io(ref e)) if e.kind() == NotFound correctly handles the normal case where no snapshot file exists yet (e.g., before any persist_plan call)

Match arm Err(err) catches CorruptRecord (malformed JSON), Io errors other than NotFound (permission denied, disk full), and any other AppError variants, logging all with milestone_id and error message
tracing::warn uses structured fields (milestone_id, error) consistent with existing tracing usage in br_process.rs and process_backend.rs
Function return type and caller contract unchanged — callers still receive Option<String> and treat None as 'no plan_hash available'
